prompt: I extend Java's List<Integer> with a subclass `PositiveIntList` that throws IllegalArgumentException from add(value) when value < 0. Code compiles and tests on PositiveIntList pass. Is anything wrong from a SOLID perspective?

assertions.text: Names the specific mechanism: strengthening preconditions (subtype demands more from callers than parent did)
Explains the contract claim: parent accepts any Integer, subtype rejects a subset — callers written against parent now break
Notes that the compiler cannot catch this — signatures match, contracts diverge
Suggests an alternative: composition / separate non-inheriting type / a contract that makes the constraint visible to callers
